# Wednesday, August 13, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1910, the [most evenly matched game in baseball history](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOwyAQRL8Gd0Sw2IYUFNe4y7VXWtgs9kYYR0Ac-e9PSXX9dSPNaPT0fm7jzVGktHy7DW0N-5jwVSLWinm8P-NJKURXaU_puU2Yy4SllhEEdFwYLtXoHcVzbBtvAyKgatBK3XVCXzsQzWo99HJ2fbhegw6gWy9RKjBojJ6FRteQfb8JI5UUopPm4rDv9RR66bX0wSnWCvRU93y575TqiiGe-4H5Mu9bE-1a66Mw9cVgYDC4PK90oKeUKC3lvWEwgJCKwSAMg-GT6kqFe3dySnxyBScXI68rZuRUeD0pLX8rBkOTrX-8KEYqvWatWDZH8YNQ_-XtsPAbAAD__-FbgbI) was played between the Pittsburgh Pirates and the Brooklyn Superbas, now the Dodgers. The two teams played to an 8-8 tie, with both having exactly 38 at-bats, 13 hits, 12 assists, two errors, five strikeouts, three walks, one passed ball, and one hit-by-pitch in what George F. Will called _“_ the baseball game of perfect symmetry.” In today’s _Science_ section, we’re talking about a [brand-new cat color](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DHO4yAUBODT4I4IsAGnoNjGXbbd0sLwcF6EIWueE_n2v5Ir_PVIo_nm322-ecxY1r9-A0epzgXeLQMR7PPjyCeWlD1hLeXYFtjbAo3arITSXIxc9nP0mM956KJLAAr6Dpy0Wgt71Up0dxfSYE00sCRQozJaW5OufrF-kaCtGjp0nzYxyl4KoeV48WCMXZKR0cqYfM8GARGp7pdHxUJ3SPmsL9gvoW5ddneiZ2P9H6YmpqbtDJ5i8Z-QqWnJdW1MTR8VU9MKBQgD3w76oviKL2g8eGrc8-YznTzj_6PuGICH6omHmuuxd7uLzzfmjM1YNoh185i_A-hXr72c-gkAAP__ygiEfw) scientists have just discovered, with a striking “salty licorice” look. If you’re a cat person, what’s your cat’s quirkiest trait? Reply to this email to let us know. We're sponsored today by [GoldCo](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0Tmv2zAMAOBfI28KdEQ-Bg8FXHfKAzoV6GLooBy-6ggsOUb-feEeQ4dOjyNBfiTBb7flpjFgWt90hLH6vCQ4SoBaYVve9_DC5IOumFPao4GtGCi1LIIJRVlPuVycxvBaro0bPYAA2cDIO6VYNyjBmvvo-64fvPBKWym8FVfbG8MHyyTXppeiwfHUWM8lZ0zx_qKhbTvjW-467ryW5MrAYc3b5T1jqnfw4ZWfsF1sjk0Y77U-CpGfiJiJmI_juDy2XMFWTGt8Ff3EtBYKUWM4O4iYDSRaKmCiHpNOFnWgdsOChYLJ-QfteCfU6cnZJiKnXinaDopeB8GIaEMhcmKM088nSuff69AbONREtMVbdEROHeNf7-yM76Z9-0JEu9e4WB0fGtf034IIDvdI5PRL_5Msed8sEDn9M6zZRvc4MAQsbUeubP17ZFM_9MvnKH4GAAD__8YBsNA), which is offering a free retirement savings guide from economist [Ben Stein](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0Tmv2zAMAOBfI28KdEQ-Bg8FXHfKAzoV6GLooBy-6ggsOUb-feEeQ4dOjyNBfiTBb7flpjFgWt90hLH6vCQ4SoBaYVve9_DC5IOumFPao4GtGCi1LIIJRVlPuVycxvBaro0bPYAA2cDIO6VYNyjBmvvo-64fvPBKWym8FVfbG8MHyyTXppeiwfHUWM8lZ0zx_qKhbTvjW-467ryW5MrAYc3b5T1jqnfw4ZWfsF1sjk0Y77U-CpGfiJiJmI_juDy2XMFWTGt8Ff3EtBYKUWM4O4iYDSRaKmCiHpNOFnWgdsOChYLJ-QfteCfU6cnZJiKnXinaDopeB8GIaEMhcmKM088nSuff69AbONREtMVbdEROHeNf7-yM76Z9-0JEu9e4WB0fGtf034IIDvdI5PRL_5Msed8sEDn9M6zZRvc4MAQsbUeubP17ZFM_9MvnKH4GAAD__8YBsNA).
|
---
## July Inflation Report Sparks Market Rally
U.S. consumer prices rose [2.7% in July from a year earlier](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OpDAUBODTmAzkXwyBg12tyHrTCZHBz_Baxm7Z7u7h9iMmmAtMXFKpvvq4zTeLAeP23x5gqk9zhHcJUCvk-f4MJ0YfbMUU4_NYIJcFSi0zp1y1dGiZmJ3FcM6yccYDcBANGKaVonpUnDa74RqUXLxmng5O90yDHaTwnukRoFdLg-ZqowMTjFLFhs5C3-vF98xp5rwVRFJwWFPu7glj3cGHM70gd2s6mmD2Wh-FiD-ET4RPdlkvQbelKyZ8-vssGKEUwqcfSwufD1gruBbjmsEWcG0JuO01nO2lJnwqNeWTiAkdEf8Yl3KgchybbNzjjSFg6TWRdDsshu8h9VfvvQz_CgAA__8D14UX), slightly below forecasts and unchanged from June, while core inflation increased 3.1% with housing costs leading gains. Food prices were flat, energy declined, and some tariff-affected goods saw modest increases. The softer-than-expected reading boosted market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut in September, and [stocks rallied](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OqzAUxfHVmM7IHxh4hYvX0GXaKZHxvQ5OjB3hmyB2P2K2MPWRfkf6f9_mm4sp5vuX29BSKHPGoyYkwn1-vNMZc0iOYsn5vS241wUr1VkJZbgYudQzuJjOuWvABkSFukErB2PE8M8o0azWuN5r6HwYuwUGrYSWUqCBTmkBi4cm2ksTo9RSCCPH1mHfD0voJQwSgtOsEwiRyt4-Ssy0Ykhn-eDe-rI1ya5Er8r0f6YmpqbjOFqfF3-NTE0XzdQkRqYmKZmaKhX_5Jvbn0icCriTp_hB_n6BI6y8XWlLzW7hdcSUYu0H1on75mL6vaM_NfpY9RMAAP__6DJ6pg) on the news, sending the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record highs and lifting the Dow nearly 500 points.
## 30-Day Federal Takeover of D.C. Begins
President Trump began a 30-day federal takeover of Washington’s police force on Tuesday, citing a crime emergency as 800 National Guard members arrived in the city. [See photos here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOhCAUhp8GOyaAF7Sg2IZutt3SoByUGRQCxzG-_cZ9ha2_5PsvP8_xaXzw-_JtNlDo4rjDWQIgQh5fR7j87oJBH_f92CbIZYKCZRRMtJT1lNejNT5cY1NZ5QAE1BUoLtuWyaEVrFqVnLrBdl3jhraxTErnDBMc-nkwfQPzUHl121jPa85Yy_uHga6Tk-u4ldw6U5OGgfUY8-MV_Y4ruHDFD-THHLcqqBUxFVJ_EaGJ0PuVYsEbEaFvMRGa9URoLojQR6H3PiK0nWmKwc9A59WDo5u5YqYpgF2AliOlmJFipJiPLVE0b7gj6QnUZKArZLjpGfObYlwAV8hE6Corm04fgi-dJA1bNuPDX0_817kfJX4DAAD__8Fmj7E). Mayor Muriel Bowser pledged [to work with federal officials](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE9DdTT4I0p2ocJBoCZ4aVKkFFbk8nkfVqRA0lZ8-0C5QroBBhjM9_3-WD6AmNLnT9hwajEvCY_K2BqW5evJb0qRoVFO6bmtWOqKtdXFKOOkukptlwDE76XvwhQRDdoOJz06p8abM6p7TE7fsDd6dcb7VcMIgw42ulvvXAS0oaPpXFNXbbVSTl8vgMMwrnHQYdQhghW9wkAtl8tXptQeGPmdX1guPm8dT4_W9irsXZhZmPk4jstW09kJM2OSzyrMfEIJM--ZqZE_45qPikXCK1Oo0jPUh4QqW3luu8TqgaFhlREDFmDpc2ols6QkgxdmhiLvd_2Dhz8k7Jw9BWG_fzs94lF_Ydm7MoX9IGaqwyh69bkB8b_H7b80vybzNwAA__-1lpRM) while noting local crime rates are already declining. The White House said federal [officers made 23 arrests](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1q7DAQBeCnkTsb_Vj-KVTcy8Vwiw3pUhppNdqdRZaMNd7Fbx8cSJM25fDBcM75uMwXixHT7c0uYCjkOcGrRCCCbX7s8cAUoiXMKe2Lg604KFRmyaWu-VALNXuL8ZjbypsAIEFVYESvNe9HLXl1N_3oRs0HULx1g9KjAG5dNwTtwfchuArN-Y0PQgnOtRgaC13Xu9AJ3wsfrGItB4-Ut-aRMdEdQjzyE7bmmpcqmjvRWpj6w-TE5GTd9WzQ3PLJTE7vOSLhtTA5RXxCva_eEpwnbfuy1tYvmOofxNSEnql_QrZaaSkUk51dVqb-QqLt-P9tgxylrjbj1xfGiKXrWctvi8X4FY5-tejTyM8AAAD__x_fiNY) and cleared dozens of homeless encampments on the first night of operations. Protests have grown alongside the federal crackdown. Adam Swart, CEO of crowd rental firm Crowds on Demand, said requests for demonstrators in Washington are [up 400% from last year](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2ynDAQhE8DmShJIH4CBU7I1qlDakCj3VkLhKVhMbd_xbvCi7uqu7_vz2N6AAXanr9hRcs-ThueOSAzpul9hIs2H4ApbtuxzpjyjJnzpKU2QvZC1ZMDCtfUlM56RI11iVZ1xshuMFqWL9sM0Pl20WbocQY_1ENbNwYB-1YOoExJ9m6TvaqVlEb1FWDbdrNvleuU81AXjURHHFP1jrTxC3244gdTtcS1DPbFvOei_lXosdDjeZ6Vj_9vijsv9HjkQo9uETuQE3uKjJkxiYT_jptF5CM9UcBKTnA61j0Ljw4TBMHwF-8hsRBfYo-BFhRLiqcTS1x32K4yWbefFALltisa-VyBwvcv_pHMj9VfAQAA__9J5o2i) and that the vast majority of attendees at political events are paid or participating as part of their work duties.
## America's Best Selling Cars in 2025, So Far
America’s [top-selling vehicles in the first half of 2025](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL_GdEa2wRgKF9fQ5dor0Qavw0YLRLYJyt-fuF-4fjRv5v3cphsQ0_b4hhV9ifu04ZkZS8E0PQ_-0BYZCu3bdqx3TPmOueTJKGOl6qVupgDEn6mtgo-IBpsKvXbWKjdYo6rF92079G7utR4sOGdsDDaAc0PnnDWzrshfbarXjVbK6r4G7Dp3j50OTocIjWgVBip7qp87bWXByJ_9jame97Viv5TyyqL5EmYUZjzPs35TPoBneFEBplyuoDDjNV1m5OuunCFleWS5aHnhhRmr5MPrJGbKnROteqxA_Mco_xLz9uY3AAD__-uKd1s) were once again led by the Ford F-Series, which moved 412,848 units, maintaining its decades-long dominance. Close behind were the Chevrolet Silverado with 284,038 sales and the Toyota RAV4 with 239,451, the highest-ranked SUV. Nine of the top 10 sellers were pickups or SUVs, underscoring U.S. buyers’ preference for size, capability, and versatility over sedans. The Toyota Camry was the lone sedan in the top 10, while overall SUVs made up 47% of top-20 sales, pickups 37%, and sedans just 16%. See [data visualization here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL_GdEa2wRgKF9fQ5dor0Qavw0YLRLYJyt-fuF-4fjRv5v3cphsQ0_b4hhV9ifu04ZkZS8E0PQ_-0BYZCu3bdqx3TPmOueTJKGOl6qVupgDEn6mtgo-IBpsKvXbWKjdYo6rF92079G7utR4sOGdsDDaAc0PnnDWzrshfbarXjVbK6r4G7Dp3j50OTocIjWgVBip7qp87bWXByJ_9jame97Viv5TyyqL5EmYUZjzPs35TPoBneFEBplyuoDDjNV1m5OuunCFleWS5aHnhhRmr5MPrJGbKnROteqxA_Mco_xLz9uY3AAD__-uKd1s).

We're sponsored today by , which is offering a free retirement savings guide from economist . | July Inflation Report Sparks Market Rally U.S. consumer prices rose , slightly below forecasts and unchanged from June, while core inflation increased 3.1% with housing costs leading gains. Food prices were flat, energy declined, and some tariff-affected goods saw modest increases. The softer-than-expected reading boosted market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut in September, and on the news, sending the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record highs and lifting the Dow nearly 500 points. 30-Day Federal Takeover of D.C. Begins President Trump began a 30-day federal takeover of Washington’s police force on Tuesday, citing a crime emergency as 800 National Guard members arrived in the city. . Mayor Muriel Bowser pledged while noting local crime rates are already declining. The White House said federal and cleared dozens of homeless encampments on the first night of operations. Protests have grown alongside the federal crackdown. Adam Swart, CEO of crowd rental firm Crowds on Demand, said requests for demonstrators in Washington are and that the vast majority of attendees at political events are paid or participating as part of their work duties. America's Best Selling Cars in 2025, So Far America’s were once again led by the Ford F-Series, which moved 412,848 units, maintaining its decades-long dominance. Close behind were the Chevrolet Silverado with 284,038 sales and the Toyota RAV4 with 239,451, the highest-ranked SUV. Nine of the top 10 sellers were pickups or SUVs, underscoring U.S. buyers’ preference for size, capability, and versatility over sedans. The Toyota Camry was the lone sedan in the top 10, while overall SUVs made up 47% of top-20 sales, pickups 37%, and sedans just 16%. See . ## Texas Supreme Court Delays Ruling
The Texas Supreme Court [rejected Gov. Greg Abbott’s request](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkkFvoz4UxD8NuRHZBoM5cCD_lqb5J2nTdtuKiwX2S0xj49QYCPvpV-lxr3t7eqP5STOajx3f1a1uu9O-NpD769HyDqZeg_fgOBhwJ-jE3HvrjIPeDk5ArywniNAQsRBHXNatnnm8kHmDZIYXkOOUUsQIy9hC5bihEksBKGGUQsIoYzWkkUxTimiakUWb32CI4QhjhCOyTNMGU5KlDWRNHEscxAhk661begUernV_1LMdwS2FNQudK-8vfRAVASkDUk7TtJxrZe1NDUh5ixOQsna-FRpu5w8i7IeLAwOhsIPz4WS70Ic4ISiOY0KXyhsdROVpEMIOnQcXRHc4IMlpEMAdHMG5n1-9fkFivUu2cxbJSAwVycaKXPXW7Mfmlf3t4H17CqK74lAUxcbzVJ23uM46o-RGN2fsH5vDrJwsw2n_8j_tnjYJ-vJVjc5fnGyfv5_vp_WU_SdWuPDm3lYS6HnzNZZHhpLx12Oi08mvInyonsj1U612Q7YW7INeNI2LFQnv47P84P3ndt-kbwdZGfVgzkV5eInf1LR6h7fqkf_uw4dsk3yb-PoOs-QHOL42C5fLy9Rq3fZJGsToZOpW_7Tv_20zY07-BAAA___vudX2) for a 48-hour ruling to oust House Democrats who fled the state over redistricting. Instead, they set a three-week briefing period, giving both sides until Sept. 4 to file final responses. The delay could push the fight past key election deadlines, complicating GOP hopes for quick passage of their mid-decade redrawing of the congressional map. Abbott announced Tuesday he plans to continue calling special sessions, [vowing no reprieve](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uxCAUBODT4I4VPOMFCoo07jZtSgvMW4MExgKyP7ePvFdIPaNPmvm5LTcbU9y3b5vR9Ne9LDs-W8LesS6YsW64r-_WS80VW_mtK7ZQFmAwUaYoHxdvY3ovYvDGMa_5gIbLaWIKlFZDMAhs0l4iu3urmbpyoZ3gKzAupOPgh2hOjCk-cs74CBcpHZ9AS4faCeE5EQx97KVeesCOL9vu6V0eWC9ryUMyofejkfGLwExg7gFDTOnMCMyhZDwHEZhXmw8bt53APAmhQFzpB6NbeVDrXOmdtgPXaBNt2FosZ3Woxh_PmFJsV0kE27KNH3zo_7vrYeAvAAD__5QKfy4) for Democrats who fled to block the GOP move that could give Republicans five new congressional seats next year.
## Abbott Activates Emergency Resources
Gov. Greg Abbott activated [state emergency resources](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOszAUhJ_GdEa-cStc_A1d_nZLZOMDWDIcZJ8lyduvkpXyAlvPaDTf93Wbbi6meKz_3Q6WHgtOB9xLAiLIE-yQVzjmZyHMe4aC33mGsuGkhGq46LnUU3AxPSdTBetFGGQFVnZNI3rVD321WeNbIwalwMDitB9aLVRotOvaVomgfRXta0z0UksppFZ113nZqKHzMHhjgmRGQIiEuaYNCB6uLOmJF-R6xr1KdiM6C9P_mBqZGle86nepXvFianzhMDWeWOg3hXxg5s57JOJupng5gsILOQL-IeYZyolHAf7B5m4DFzguvMAFGfjbCqctg6Mq23DeY0qxtB0zYt1dTO-D9Detl1U_AQAA__9vfJDm) this week as a major storm system threatens Texas with severe weather and flash flooding. Rescue boats, helicopters, and road crews are on standby as [the system moves](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL_upCAUhZ8GOwwgIhYU29jNtlsa_lwHEhQDd8eZt9-4xbzArzvJSU6-7_x5rA-bcjqev-0OBt9bWQ-4WgZEqCvsUJ9w-E_DUvcKrfytHlosq2BipExTPqzBpvxZZReMY2HmHRg-jSPTQs-6i0YzxYJSCrTchHPOe8Wd26RVOuhZ8S6Ze4xpPnDO-CD6aXJ8FPPkYHZSBk4kg5Cw1B4jILxt2_KnvKD2vuxdNhHxbGT4RcRCxHJdV-9jLcfdErHcOkQstmLyGe7kXEGk1mN6WYRGv5b0a0htBBvolm2LVNxsWvL-jGdXTTivlHNqaiKSPXeb8n8O_Nl7LyP-BQAA__9dp4XI) into Northwest Texas before it is projected to sweep to Southeast Texas by the end of the week. Forecasters warn the storms could bring several inches of rain, strong winds, and frequent lightning, raising the risk of dangerous flash floods. Abbott [urged Texans](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGupCAURL8Gd7cDqA9csOiNm8mb7SwNyLUhQTFwW7v_fmJPMj_wdpVUclKn_nxP3zamuD1-2xUNvZY8bXjWhERYJlyxPHCb35VyWQvW_Cwz1pAnyWUPXINoJ29jek9d443jfhANGqH6nmupB90E04svpcUsVMedWGaJi5a6a61Sg8DWYhPNBeNatEJw0cqbUk70clAOB9d1XrCOo4-Uy40CEr5sXdI7H1huc16bZALRXll7Z3JkcjzP87bW7eqYHHGDZ2VyvKSYHD_ZOpeJwM4UD0tYoZIlhP-yULDueasINqD1kBf4-FfA144zoQfKsOYDgULJz0cACviPcuEL3O_iV0qDa4rx-xlTivVLsY4_VhvTZzX97OvDyL8BAAD__4smlps) to monitor local forecasts, follow official guidance, and avoid driving through flooded roads.
## Robb Elementary Documents Released
Uvalde County released more than [1,500 pages of documents](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpiAUhq8GO77AUQYtKLahm223NPwclSzKBM6n491v3FuY9nmT9-_P5_zpUk7H-tvtaOh7KfOBV8tIhHXGHeuKR7gblbpXbOVdA7atzCBAcTFy2c_RpXzPQxeNF3GSHRqplRIjjNPYbQZUcEGqZYih18MA0utFOlD-w2s1Odcl85iJUfZSCtnDS2svFUza4-SHIUo2CIyJSn3RhoTfri35LifWVyh7l81G9NVY_4uBZWCv63r9bY4ekYF91jCwD-HpOLFRWh3hw55YBlaMDKwEBvZ9uhyRh_I-6OYVM7qGjVcMpcbGl1p2Xov3HDPueJCrN29bKZSOlYHtqolfV8o5tQ_NBrHuLuX_Helnz54G_gUAAP__p9yPCQ), along with nearly seven hours of video footage, from the 2022 Robb Elementary shooting that killed 19 children and two teachers. The release comes less than a month after a Texas appeals court [sided with media organizations](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0TGupDAMBuDThM6jJANDKCi2Sfe23RKFYIZoAx45Znhz-1X2Cq-0bH3Wr__P1_QVUk7H83fYcZTvlaYDr5JRBHnCHfmJR_wUId4ZC50csWw0WW070A7MfVpCyp-pbZZx1stgGhxN33XaWTe4ZqtDRB2NaSOiQ9s9Zu0G92iN63s9zE0aK6aduRujzd3e-n42nR36GYe5bRejWo1LEuKbbCj4HcqaP_RGvkXamzxuIq-i7r-U9cr667puf0uQulTW1zTK-kwxZGV9faWs172y3jyU9eH1wpALRDpZgM-MBdIBa3gTA61QKTAWSDZkqBrQKRmlwEoMjBlDwXpZ4kaUK3TIBxgj8VJgZdrhfIe8IJSNSNLxVNY3PC6vK-WcyqNXrX7uIeX_eeRnLbxH-y8AAP__H6ad-Q) in a 2022 lawsuit seeking school and county records about the shooting. Families of the victims, who also advocated for the release, say the transparency is overdue and essential for accountability. The Texas Department of Public Safety [continues to fight](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbHOozAQhJ8Guo1sAz9QUFyT7r_2SrS2l2Bp7Y1sk_y8_Yno3uDa3dF8M5o_3-s3Bg7p8RsjLfVnkzXRuzDVSnmlSPlByZ2lSo6ZihzZUdllNcoMoCbQ3eox8Ln2rV-s8rNuadHjMKjJTPPU7gtpNY9GIyo3qqHHqVPK0tzZ2fnN0FcblstMTbrTWunO3MbR6sHMo6XZ9r3XTa_Ihyr5Vneq9INl41NelG9OYsvLXuuzNN2vxtwbc7_i9wUTpiopyKX5d23MncUhN-Z-vJA9gZMj1RNk24ILyAUyMWEhyGItEFOkVDGfUHaRGtIDQqqUEzJ4ccf1LWDFnw4jvIIngQ8BLhwkwgyRwOUQCRjf8DwsBwcFN6pnmxf_fAfmUL7GplePiIE_ler_DfFazN8AAAD__11lpBQ) against similar lawsuits in a separate legal battle over releasing its records.
| Flying together with our sponsor [](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM3OpCAQRZ8GdxoKscEFi0k6vevZzpKAlEoCYgD75-0ndk_mBb7tvZW6J-fPXd-ND35bfpuIqr7mpDd8loC1YtYYMS-4Te9SU44ZSzryhGVNmlE2tFS20GtnfHhr3jhlqRuhQQViGKhkcpTNqjgMs52FkVKA4MBmMxvmmKF2gplZaLw6n1EJPQCFnnVCWBjYKCyOlnMHhFN0vqbc1RUrvkyZwzs9MHdTik1Qa617If0vwm6E3fz2wFK7yHy3hGRNOOP-dtSov_ikv2I0PhB2OcOIzh-R9NfdeNfuJtcNc1n9_q-fTNyNX7bPxf8SJG05Ffqr4Nh8NZpRPsJwoULyJiu3P30IvlwE4XQ5Bz-49WeSH4r9DQAA__--TJFi) **What the U.S. (and Investors) Learned From Israel v. Iran** When Iran threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz, a gateway for 20% of the world’s oil, it reminded us why the U.S. keeps up to 714M barrels in the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve. As for the critical minerals we rely on for national defense and consumer electronics, the situation is far worse. For example, China controls 80% of the global lithium supply, but the U.S. has no strategic reserves of these critical minerals. [**M2i Global**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM3OpCAQRZ8GdxoKscEFi0k6vevZzpKAlEoCYgD75-0ndk_mBb7tvZW6J-fPXd-ND35bfpuIqr7mpDd8loC1YtYYMS-4Te9SU44ZSzryhGVNmlE2tFS20GtnfHhr3jhlqRuhQQViGKhkcpTNqjgMs52FkVKA4MBmMxvmmKF2gplZaLw6n1EJPQCFnnVCWBjYKCyOlnMHhFN0vqbc1RUrvkyZwzs9MHdTik1Qa617If0vwm6E3fz2wFK7yHy3hGRNOOP-dtSov_ikv2I0PhB2OcOIzh-R9NfdeNfuJtcNc1n9_q-fTNyNX7bPxf8SJG05Ffqr4Nh8NZpRPsJwoULyJiu3P30IvlwE4XQ5Bz-49WeSH4r9DQAA__--TJFi) plans to fix that. They’re shoring up the U.S.’s critical mineral supplies by rolling out a three-tiered approach – revitalizing abandoned mines, recycling reclaimed scrap metal, and forging public-private partnerships across industry, academia, and government at all levels. [**The federal government is actively working with them**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM3OpCAQRZ8GdxoKscEFi0k6vevZzpKAlEoCYgD75-0ndk_mBb7tvZW6J-fPXd-ND35bfpuIqr7mpDd8loC1YtYYMS-4Te9SU44ZSzryhGVNmlE2tFS20GtnfHhr3jhlqRuhQQViGKhkcpTNqjgMs52FkVKA4MBmMxvmmKF2gplZaLw6n1EJPQCFnnVCWBjYKCyOlnMHhFN0vqbc1RUrvkyZwzs9MHdTik1Qa617If0vwm6E3fz2wFK7yHy3hGRNOOP-dtSov_ikv2I0PhB2OcOIzh-R9NfdeNfuJtcNc1n9_q-fTNyNX7bPxf8SJG05Ffqr4Nh8NZpRPsJwoULyJiu3P30IvlwE4XQ5Bz-49WeSH4r9DQAA__--TJFi) at Nevada’s Hawthorne Army Depot to launch the first phase of a U.S. Strategic Mineral Reserve. They recently secured a $850M copper offtake agreement. It’s not hard to understand why M2i is already publicly traded. But now that M2i Global’s finalized a reverse takeover of Volato Group, they’re preparing to uplist onto the NYSE and open even bigger doors. Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Texas Supreme Court Delays Ruling The Texas Supreme Court for a 48-hour ruling to oust House Democrats who fled the state over redistricting. Instead, they set a three-week briefing period, giving both sides until Sept. 4 to file final responses. The delay could push the fight past key election deadlines, complicating GOP hopes for quick passage of their mid-decade redrawing of the congressional map. Abbott announced Tuesday he plans to continue calling special sessions, for Democrats who fled to block the GOP move that could give Republicans five new congressional seats next year. Abbott Activates Emergency Resources Gov. Greg Abbott activated this week as a major storm system threatens Texas with severe weather and flash flooding. Rescue boats, helicopters, and road crews are on standby as into Northwest Texas before it is projected to sweep to Southeast Texas by the end of the week. Forecasters warn the storms could bring several inches of rain, strong winds, and frequent lightning, raising the risk of dangerous flash floods. Abbott to monitor local forecasts, follow official guidance, and avoid driving through flooded roads. Robb Elementary Documents Released Uvalde County released more than , along with nearly seven hours of video footage, from the 2022 Robb Elementary shooting that killed 19 children and two teachers. The release comes less than a month after a Texas appeals court in a 2022 lawsuit seeking school and county records about the shooting. Families of the victims, who also advocated for the release, say the transparency is overdue and essential for accountability. The Texas Department of Public Safety against similar lawsuits in a separate legal battle over releasing its records. | Flying together with our sponsor What the U.S. (and Investors) Learned From Israel v. Iran When Iran threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz, a gateway for 20% of the world’s oil, it reminded us why the U.S. keeps up to 714M barrels in the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve. As for the critical minerals we rely on for national defense and consumer electronics, the situation is far worse. For example, China controls 80% of the global lithium supply, but the U.S. has no strategic reserves of these critical minerals. plans to fix that. They’re shoring up the U.S.’s critical mineral supplies by rolling out a three-tiered approach – revitalizing abandoned mines, recycling reclaimed scrap metal, and forging public-private partnerships across industry, academia, and government at all levels. at Nevada’s Hawthorne Army Depot to launch the first phase of a U.S. Strategic Mineral Reserve. They recently secured a $850M copper offtake agreement. It’s not hard to understand why M2i is already publicly traded. But now that M2i Global’s finalized a reverse takeover of Volato Group, they’re preparing to uplist onto the NYSE and open even bigger doors. Even better? () ➤ Austin: Police are investigating a $30,000 paintball scavenger hunt that sent a bystander to the ER, an event allegedly organized by social media live streamer Ice Poseidon. () ➤ Ingram: The city council approved installing three flood sirens between Ingram Dam and Riverview Road east of the dam, though costs, funding, and an installation date remain unresolved. ------------------------------------------------------------

Tuesday, Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows formally rejected the federal Department of Justice’s (DOJ) request for access to the state’s voter rolls and other election-related information. This comes shortly after the Secretary of State told the DOJ to “go jump in the Gulf of Maine” in response to their initial request for these records. [...]

name: Ben Samuels
subject: Mutually assured gerrymandering
body:
The Great Gerrymander War of 2025 isn't necessarily that bad for Democrats. But it's terrible for Americans.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.L8RaqqG47jtZbu1SXhT10fwZJGDpwvUop5P8ZHDnNoA?) for more
---
# [Mutually assured gerrymandering](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTUwODk1NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzY4MTU0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-5vMf8zAcY6WSuFZKlcRJVt3bJ_QEz8a6U_UIdv95Mg)
### The Great Gerrymander War of 2025 isn't necessarily that bad for Democrats. But it's terrible for Americans.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Aug 13
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NTA4OTU0NiwiZXhwIjoxNzU3NjgxNTQ2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.rTJkqj6pSLi0mWQeSbx1TyI-XsBUuGPCHtDDqxC5ecI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTUwODk1NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzY4MTU0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-5vMf8zAcY6WSuFZKlcRJVt3bJ_QEz8a6U_UIdv95Mg&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTUwODk1NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzY4MTU0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-5vMf8zAcY6WSuFZKlcRJVt3bJ_QEz8a6U_UIdv95Mg)
---
| | [](https://substack..4SCk-IHP43XU8cr04hWX9rMDslG3LGRIdvfMVML2ojM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/exhausting-fruitless-gerrymanders?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

For the last few years, I've joked that I'm one of the very few people to have ever been personally gerrymandered: I ran for Congress in '22 and [dropped out of the race](https://substack.com/redirect/93684987-d9b7-4211-bce4-2d0f73f90321?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after Missouri's legislature was careful to carve my house out of the district I was running in.
I was annoyed and more than a bit bitter, but Missouri isn't in the business of making things easy for Democrats any more than Illinois makes things easy for Republicans.
The solution [I suggested at the time](https://substack.com/redirect/913aa255-c708-40bd-aacd-10f59d64d47d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was for Democratic and Republican states to come together to push for fairer districts without losing a partisan edge.
For example: [Illinois](https://substack.com/redirect/04d3de14-561d-4970-890c-9f908d48b237?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has 17 seats controlled by Democrats, and [Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/36add154-861d-4a88-b316-2ae835ed0943?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Oklahoma](https://substack.com/redirect/9becd6a8-f021-44cc-8f48-63b5b24560fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [Arkansas](https://substack.com/redirect/851f92f8-3ca7-48c6-ba79-f6da9cc21f51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) together have 17 seats controlled by Republicans. Governors partner on stuff all the time;1 maybe they could get creative in supporting better policy without helping the other party.
That's something we can think about in 10 years, I mused. Nothing we can do until then.
How naive I was. Now, states across the country--[Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/a1002cb7-13b3-4581-b513-9e5990e932f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [California](https://substack.com/redirect/ac56b5ec-40f1-4b6b-99c7-1638f21c2bee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Ohio](https://substack.com/redirect/47969fba-e973-4006-bbee-1474f12b00a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [New York](https://substack.com/redirect/c82d687a-518c-40fd-9348-931bc9f1da36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/595aa4fc-cc5b-4c73-92b4-eff29d843bed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Illinois](https://substack.com/redirect/ce7c8402-d362-4f51-a4ac-e0bc0ac71049?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Florida](https://substack.com/redirect/2315fb37-7cbf-4f6c-a2cc-a92357bfe6ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), etc.--are pushing for mid-decade gerrymanders to give their preferred parties an edge.2
That's the opposite of partnership. It's brinksmanship. It's mutually assured destruction.
When the dust settles on this fracas, **it 's unlikely to generate meaningful gains for either party. The only loser? The American people.**
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU1MDg5NTQ2LCJleHAiOjIwNzA2NjU1NDYsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.lWV_Slg6mXDkUeB8Z9zs83mZvgST_fhadPhep5ZoDu0?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _The redistricting wars playing out around the country likely won 't be the deciding factor for control of Congress after the '26 elections._
> * _Democrats have no choice but to retaliate, and meet gerrymanders with gerrymanders. That 's really bad for voters, and it will increase political polarization._
> * _Across this whole process, no one is even pretending to do what 's best for their constituents._
>

### A brief recap of this redistricting mess
Here's a quick rundown of what's been happening over the past few weeks:
1. Republicans face an [uphill climb to hold onto their narrow majority](https://substack.com/redirect/44eff237-84c5-468f-9768-d0ef7ed87a1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. House of Representatives.
2. After [Governor Greg Abbott was cajoled by Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/72fca1b5-b738-41db-8044-16fc8373e346?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Texas decided to redraw its Congressional maps.
3. Texas' map would (likely) lead to [five more Republican U.S. House seats](https://substack.com/redirect/ab78d2eb-261f-4ed2-b490-bcb41a7304dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but [Texas House Democrats left the state](https://substack.com/redirect/3d751b0d-98bd-4da4-b2ac-ffcc1898a5f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to deny Republicans the quorum necessary to pass laws. (This just delays Republican aspirations; it will very likely still happen, sooner or later.)
4. [Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/3c684727-d5b7-4ea6-a941-d86badff14ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Indiana](https://substack.com/redirect/85b41927-42ae-4ef0-9a77-86dcdc26724b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and other Republican states might also try to redraw their Congressional districts to help Republicans.
5. In response, [California](https://substack.com/redirect/122efda4-f706-4c9f-921e-23e3a6b1b145?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [New York](https://substack.com/redirect/92ba67b8-6099-40ab-9128-936dc36f7b10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Illinois](https://substack.com/redirect/420c7c02-de25-4d52-95cb-ad9d0169a2d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [others](https://substack.com/redirect/09e74999-66ad-4292-b421-a0b325fe05de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are threatening to do the same on the Democratic side, meeting aggressive gerrymander with aggressive gerrymander.

### Gerrymandering is nothing new
There are lots of problems with what's happening, but I'll start with a tactical one: there isn't that much left to gerrymander. Let's look at Illinois, Colorado, North Carolina, and Michigan as examples.3
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ad0fce2c-1235-4a2e-a0fa-6e9efb59c16d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It'd be hard for Illinois and North Carolina to do too much more than they already have. And in states like Colorado and Michigan that have nonpartisan redistricting processes, their legislatures have no power to try.
Just based on their partisan lean, Illinois' and Colorado's Congressional representation should look pretty similar. The same is true of North Carolina and Michigan.4 But when politicians get to draw maps, they'll never be fair or representative.
Democrats in North Carolina and Republicans in Illinois deserve better than what they're getting.5
I'm yelling into the wind when I say this, but I'll say it anyway: partisan redistricting is terrible policy. The problem is that **there is an enormous disconnect between what 's good for voters and the incentives of politicians trying to advance their careers**.
If Congressional Democrats from Illinois and Congressional Republicans from North Carolina voted to end gerrymandering, they'd be voting themselves out of a job. That selfishness is a huge part of the reason why the elected officials who have the structural ability to make change consistently fail to do so.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTUwODk1NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzY4MTU0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-5vMf8zAcY6WSuFZKlcRJVt3bJ_QEz8a6U_UIdv95Mg)
### When the dust settles, redistricting wars (probably) won't lead to a huge Republican edge
Here's my extremely rough, it's-bound-to-change accounting for what we're likely to see as a result of these gerrymanders around the country:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/18e68063-109c-4c15-a6c6-6d4f48962135?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A _lot_ of caveats here, but chief among them: this is all extremely fluid.6 Maybe other states will join in on this whole donnybrook; maybe Texas and California will reach some sort of detente; maybe courts will intervene in unexpected ways.
Is it possible that this margin decides the majority? Absolutely. But it's highly unlikely; **swings from election to election[tend to be larger than just five or 10 seats in the U.S. House](https://substack.com/redirect/12ba9b0d-e412-4224-89e1-835e12211b07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. The economy, healthcare cuts, and Trump's popularity will all be far more consequential.
But let's call this what it is: an unusual, and unusually brazen, power grab from Republicans.
### The losers here? The American people
Democrats are right to be up-in-arms about all of this, and they're right to retaliate--unilateral disarmament is not a workable strategy.
But the American people lose out here. Why? More gerrymandering leads to fewer competitive races and fewer Americans whose votes really matter.
There are 435 seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. Of those 435, [only 40 seats](https://substack.com/redirect/fd443165-bcb5-4db3-a5a6-1c4260419339?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--just 9%!--are likely to be competitive. **That means that 91% of us --including basically everyone in Missouri--votes in an election where the outcome is all but decided**.
After this latest round of gerrymanders, it'll be even fewer.
### Who cares about what's good for those pesky voters?
It's worth noting how these sorts of things get decided, and how little consideration there is for the voters. Here's [what I wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/913aa255-c708-40bd-aacd-10f59d64d47d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about Missouri's legislative process in '22:
> The absurdities of Missouri's redistricting process were myriad: a state senator drawing a new congressional district for [his incumbent cousin](https://substack.com/redirect/6ec0a2fe-b961-458a-8fe0-2f5abc886070?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); a [months-long filibuster](https://substack.com/redirect/c92a7748-e548-472e-85c6-41d961c0c0a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Missouri's [seven-member Conservative Caucus](https://substack.com/redirect/6b488c91-d70e-4d43-a4cf-76b8ddce2b71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), three of whom are running for Congress and held up the redistricting process to draw themselves better maps; a Democrat [who voted in favor of the new map](https://substack.com/redirect/583a0beb-2106-4bcb-bf84-e25d4484faca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- one of very few to do so -- that just so happens to make [his primary](https://substack.com/redirect/69df06f5-027f-4f0f-b144-12ebeae37858?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) challenge against Rep. Cori Bush (D-Mo.) [much stronger](https://substack.com/redirect/f2030fa6-6139-4e30-944e-570c581892fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
And that's not unique. Here's what [political reporter Dave Drebes](https://substack.com/redirect/69d99d3d-127f-4ba0-a586-800cd7e5dfb0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) just wrote about [Missouri's latest process](https://substack.com/redirect/9ddf4226-f906-4510-abea-c39ec09e6089?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The changes of the quickie redistricting map ([see the current map here, BTW](https://substack.com/redirect/52327d27-b2ba-43c4-9734-35156bcc1b5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) follow logically from one big fact: the last redistricting needed to accommodate **Blaine Luetkemeyer** 's mid-MO residence. But [Missouri's third congressional district] is now represented by **Bob Onder** from St. Charles.
> * That allows [MO-3] to become more compact, more western. It's more of a St. Charles district.
>

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=170698271&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTUwODk1NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzY4MTU0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-5vMf8zAcY6WSuFZKlcRJVt3bJ_QEz8a6U_UIdv95Mg)
Unless you live in a state with nonpartisan redistricting, the decisions about where to draw your district lines probably stem from the same horse trading.
All of this is truly an exercise in politicians advancing their own agendas and ambition, voters be damned.
### Competition is good
[87% of Americans say](https://substack.com/redirect/9da98101-ed5d-4e72-b588-52ad2f4811bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that political polarization is a threat to the country. They're not wrong: the political parties have never been [so ideologically fractured](https://substack.com/redirect/27bc01eb-e2d4-47d7-a5e2-214b9d16b0c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Gerrymandering is a huge part of the reason for this. When only 9% of U.S. House races will see competitive general elections, 91% are functionally decided in primaries.
Primary elections are low-turnout affairs, and primary voters are more partisan. In practice, that means **the vast majority of members of Congress only have to worry about keeping their primary voters happy** , since that's their only hurdle to reelection.
If you're a Democrat in a super Republican district: tough luck. Your member of Congress has no real incentive to pay attention to you.
Here's another notable piece of polling data: when asked to give a one-word description of U.S. politics, Americans' [two most popular choices](https://substack.com/redirect/d6c637c6-f9f9-49a1-ae34-f28c20968648?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) were "divisive" and "corrupt."
Who can blame us? The gerrymandering process around the country presents an open-and-shut case of divisiveness and corruption.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTcwNjk4MjcxLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU1MDg5NTQ2LCJleHAiOjIwNzA2NjU1NDYsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.lWV_Slg6mXDkUeB8Z9zs83mZvgST_fhadPhep5ZoDu0?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/eefa66ff-a6e6-4b36-b382-38ed631a9315?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
When I worked for Governor Charlie Baker in Massachusetts, I worked on interstate policies that involved interacting with governors and governor's offices across the region. At least in private, most governors--regardless of party--are collaborative and happy to work with one another. (In public, maybe less so. We're left unable to [reveal the best in our elected officials](https://substack.com/redirect/84c278d6-86c8-457c-a266-a21820ff095d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
2
Once again naive on my part, but I straight-up did not even know that this was legal or possible.
3
I've made up this "Gerrymander Index," but the concept is simple. It takes the percentage that the winning candidate received in the most recent presidential election and subtracts that from the percentage of the state's Congressional districts held by that party.
It's overly simplistic but gives you a sense for what's happening. It should be obvious but the data bears it out: when politicians are in control of redistricting, you end up with districts that favor the party in control. When it's run by apolitical bodies, they do a _much_ better job reflecting the actual partisan makeup of the state.
4
Congressional representation for both Colorado and Michigan leans ever-so-slightly Republican relative to how they voted in the 2024 presidential election, but a) it was a good year for Republicans, and b) that understates just how balanced they are:
* Michigan has [five Congressional districts](https://substack.com/redirect/d50291c8-4f25-473c-97b8-0a291028927f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that are highly competitive (between R+5 and D+5). Two are held by Democrats, and three are held by Republicans.
* Colorado has [three highly competitive districts](https://substack.com/redirect/d545ef57-9abc-4b5c-bec8-4e9a02e8f604?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), all held by Republicans.

5
Sadly, none of this is new or novel: gerrymandering is named after [Elbridge Gerry](https://substack.com/redirect/e8633474-ebe4-482b-ada9-43cfccc1ab00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a Founding Father who was aggressive in deploying his namesake tactic as Governor of Massachusetts in 1810.
It's been used for centuries for different partisan and nefarious purposes: to help political allies, to advance careers, and to [disenfranchise Black voters](https://substack.com/redirect/f436ad1d-161d-4b94-baf8-8107e56e093f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for instance.
6
There have been rumors that California would fully go for broke with a 52-0 map, though I think in practice that's unlikely. But California has a lot of room to work with, and I wouldn't be surprised to see something like a 50-2 map.
Then again: because of their nonpartisan redistricting commission, they have to clear changes through voters in a special election, which is hardly a given. So just in California, we could be looking at seismic differences in what the final Democratic head count will be.
That's why I've heavily caveated the table above. There's a ton that could change at any time, in one direction or another.

clean_body: The Great Gerrymander War of 2025 isn't necessarily that bad for Democrats. But it's terrible for Americans. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more The Great Gerrymander War of 2025 isn't necessarily that bad for Democrats. But it's terrible for Americans. | | | Aug 13 | | | | | | | | | | | | For the last few years, I've joked that I'm one of the very few people to have ever been personally gerrymandered: I ran for Congress in '22 and after Missouri's legislature was careful to carve my house out of the district I was running in. I was annoyed and more than a bit bitter, but Missouri isn't in the business of making things easy for Democrats any more than Illinois makes things easy for Republicans. The solution was for Democratic and Republican states to come together to push for fairer districts without losing a partisan edge. For example: has 17 seats controlled by Democrats, and , , and together have 17 seats controlled by Republicans. Governors partner on stuff all the time;1 maybe they could get creative in supporting better policy without helping the other party. That's something we can think about in 10 years, I mused. Nothing we can do until then. How naive I was. Now, states across the country--, , , , , , , etc.--are pushing for mid-decade gerrymanders to give their preferred parties an edge.2 That's the opposite of partnership. It's brinksmanship. It's mutually assured destruction. When the dust settles on this fracas, it 's unlikely to generate meaningful gains for either party. The only loser? The American people. ? Article summary: The redistricting wars playing out around the country likely won 't be the deciding factor for control of Congress after the '26 elections. Democrats have no choice but to retaliate, and meet gerrymanders with gerrymanders. That 's really bad for voters, and it will increase political polarization. Across this whole process, no one is even pretending to do what 's best for their constituents. A brief recap of this redistricting mess Here's a quick rundown of what's been happening over the past few weeks: 1. Republicans face an in the U.S. House of Representatives. 2. After , Texas decided to redraw its Congressional maps. 3. Texas' map would (likely) lead to , but to deny Republicans the quorum necessary to pass laws. (This just delays Republican aspirations; it will very likely still happen, sooner or later.) 4. , , and other Republican states might also try to redraw their Congressional districts to help Republicans. 5. In response, , , , and are threatening to do the same on the Democratic side, meeting aggressive gerrymander with aggressive gerrymander. Gerrymandering is nothing new There are lots of problems with what's happening, but I'll start with a tactical one: there isn't that much left to gerrymander. Let's look at Illinois, Colorado, North Carolina, and Michigan as examples.3 | | ---|---|--- It'd be hard for Illinois and North Carolina to do too much more than they already have. And in states like Colorado and Michigan that have nonpartisan redistricting processes, their legislatures have no power to try. Just based on their partisan lean, Illinois' and Colorado's Congressional representation should look pretty similar. The same is true of North Carolina and Michigan.4 But when politicians get to draw maps, they'll never be fair or representative. Democrats in North Carolina and Republicans in Illinois deserve better than what they're getting.5 I'm yelling into the wind when I say this, but I'll say it anyway: partisan redistricting is terrible policy. The problem is that there is an enormous disconnect between what 's good for voters and the incentives of politicians trying to advance their careers. If Congressional Democrats from Illinois and Congressional Republicans from North Carolina voted to end gerrymandering, they'd be voting themselves out of a job. That selfishness is a huge part of the reason why the elected officials who have the structural ability to make change consistently fail to do so. When the dust settles, redistricting wars (probably) won't lead to a huge Republican edge Here's my extremely rough, it's-bound-to-change accounting for what we're likely to see as a result of these gerrymanders around the country: | | ---|---|--- A lot of caveats here, but chief among them: this is all extremely fluid.6 Maybe other states will join in on this whole donnybrook; maybe Texas and California will reach some sort of detente; maybe courts will intervene in unexpected ways. Is it possible that this margin decides the majority? Absolutely. But it's highly unlikely; swings from election to election. The economy, healthcare cuts, and Trump's popularity will all be far more consequential. But let's call this what it is: an unusual, and unusually brazen, power grab from Republicans. The losers here? The American people Democrats are right to be up-in-arms about all of this, and they're right to retaliate--unilateral disarmament is not a workable strategy. But the American people lose out here. Why? More gerrymandering leads to fewer competitive races and fewer Americans whose votes really matter. There are 435 seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. Of those 435, 9%!--are likely to be competitive. That means that 91% of us --including basically everyone in Missouri--votes in an election where the outcome is all but decided. After this latest round of gerrymanders, it'll be even fewer. Who cares about what's good for those pesky voters? It's worth noting how these sorts of things get decided, and how little consideration there is for the voters. Here's about Missouri's legislative process in '22: The absurdities of Missouri's redistricting process were myriad: a state senator drawing a new congressional district for ; a by Missouri's , three of whom are running for Congress and held up the redistricting process to draw themselves better maps; a Democrat -- one of very few to do so -- that just so happens to make challenge against Rep. Cori Bush (D-Mo.) . And that's not unique. Here's what just wrote about : The changes of the quickie redistricting map () follow logically from one big fact: the last redistricting needed to accommodate Blaine Luetkemeyer 's mid-MO residence. But [Missouri's third congressional district] is now represented by Bob Onder from St. Charles. That allows [MO-3] to become more compact, more western. It's more of a St. Charles district. Unless you live in a state with nonpartisan redistricting, the decisions about where to draw your district lines probably stem from the same horse trading. All of this is truly an exercise in politicians advancing their own agendas and ambition, voters be damned. Competition is good that political polarization is a threat to the country. They're not wrong: the political parties have never been . Gerrymandering is a huge part of the reason for this. When only 9% of U.S. House races will see competitive general elections, 91% are functionally decided in primaries. Primary elections are low-turnout affairs, and primary voters are more partisan. In practice, that means the vast majority of members of Congress only have to worry about keeping their primary voters happy , since that's their only hurdle to reelection. If you're a Democrat in a super Republican district: tough luck. Your member of Congress has no real incentive to pay attention to you. Here's another notable piece of polling data: when asked to give a one-word description of U.S. politics, Americans' were "divisive" and "corrupt." Who can blame us? Two are held by Democrats, and three are held by Republicans. It's been used for centuries for different partisan and nefarious purposes: to help political allies, to advance careers, and to , for instance. 6 There have been rumors that California would fully go for broke with a 52-0 map, though I think in practice that's unlikely. But California has a lot of room to work with, and I wouldn't be surprised to see something like a 50-2 map. Then again: because of their nonpartisan redistricting commission, they have to clear changes through voters in a special election, which is hardly a given. Peter,
The brazen gerrymandering being attempted in Texas right now is just the latest example of anti-democracy extremists manipulating the system to hoard power.
They admit they’re trying to “pick up five seats” in Texas — at the expense of many communities across the state. And if they’re successful in Texas, they’re going to try to redraw maps wherever they can across the nation to stack the deck in their favor. _That’s why it’s absolutely critical that we give this fight everything we’ve got._
All On The Line has a battle-tested playbook to push back on attempt after attempt to silence the will of voters. And this latest attack is no different.
**[Every dollar you donate fuels our grassroots fight for democracy in this critical moment. Whether it’s $5, $50, or $500, you have the power to make a real impact.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113222698/563070862/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_TXGftJrny_email_20250813-AOTL-Tier1-TXGftJrny_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250813-AOTL-Tier1-TXGftJrny_&utm_campaign=TXGftJrny&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImUyODM1NDBmLTNkNzgtZjAxMS1iNDgxLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=1t_kNXnrN0a7oQnY2SmavbvpB21XJNGkGyMp4r246zM=) Here’s how:**
1\. You’ll help mobilize voters in key communities with the resources they need to make their voices heard.
2\. You’ll help educate the public on what’s happening in Texas and how it’s meant to silence the will of the people.
3\. You’ll help supply our volunteers with the educational resources they need to ramp up our rapid-response organizing whenever and wherever necessary.
4\. You’ll help train and equip team members ahead of high-stakes redistricting votes, special sessions, lawsuit decisions, and more.
5\. And you’ll fund our rapid response communications campaigns to sound the alarm to Americans that their democracy is under attack by anti-democracy extremists.

clean_body: | Peter, The brazen gerrymandering being attempted in Texas right now is just the latest example of anti-democracy extremists manipulating the system to hoard power. They admit they’re trying to “pick up five seats” in Texas — at the expense of many communities across the state. And if they’re successful in Texas, they’re going to try to redraw maps wherever they can across the nation to stack the deck in their favor. That’s why it’s absolutely critical that we give this fight everything we’ve got. All On The Line has a battle-tested playbook to push back on attempt after attempt to silence the will of voters. And this latest attack is no different. = Here’s how: 1\. You’ll help mobilize voters in key communities with the resources they need to make their voices heard. 2\. You’ll help educate the public on what’s happening in Texas and how it’s meant to silence the will of the people. 3\. You’ll help supply our volunteers with the educational resources they need to ramp up our rapid-response organizing whenever and wherever necessary. 4\. You’ll help train and equip team members ahead of high-stakes redistricting votes, special sessions, lawsuit decisions, and more. 5\. And you’ll fund our rapid response communications campaigns to sound the alarm to Americans that their democracy is under attack by anti-democracy extremists. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = — Team All On The Line is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. name: =?utf-8?b?VGhhbmsgeW91LCBQQlMg4p2k77iPICh2aWEgVlBQKQ==?=
subject: re: Corporation for Public Broadcasting SHUTS DOWN!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Breaking >>>>
| **We are DEVASTATED!!!** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92996?t=6&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz) Trump gutted $1.1 BILLION from public broadcasting — putting news, children’s shows, and educational programs on the chopping block for _years to come._

[**We will NEVER forgive Trump or his MAGA minions for destroying public media! So we’re sending PBS a letter to thank them for their decades of service — and to stand with them in this critical fight »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92996?t=7&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz) | [**CHIP IN $3 TO SIGN NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92996?t=8&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz)
---
TO: PBS
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you. To everyone at PBS,

**Thank you.** From Mr. Rogers to Sesame Street to PBS NewsHour and so much more, your freely available programming has meant so much to millions of Americans!

I am FURIOUS with Trump and the MAGA mob for wiping out your funding, but please know that I have your back in this fight.

**I also know that Trump’s attacks on the free press don’t end here — he wants to shut down MSNBC, CNN, and anyone else who holds him accountable.**

It’s not just upsetting, _it’s a dangerous step towards**outright authoritarianism!**_ That’s why I’m doing everything in my power to stop him.

[**I’m signing this letter today by chipping in $3 to VPP to thank you for your years of service and to join VPP in the fight against Trump’s attacks on the free press. It is _absolutely critical_ that we elect good Democrats who defend public media and our most essential freedoms »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92996?t=9&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz) | [**CHIP IN $3 TO SIGN NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92996?t=10&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
_\- The Voter Protection Project_
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=20905%2E878179%2EGiBCHz)

name: Defend Our Courts Team
subject: Wisconsin showed us how to win
body:
They’ve already started moving money into Pennsylvania — quietly, so as not to repeat the mistakes of Wisconsin. The media isn’t covering it. Most voters don’t even know it’s happening.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m_VCtkjlGYcymjXbTG00wzRkoLgvGvNpa7zuiv6mSX9w541GgA7Rkc9gn30x3F0LRD89ZrpYthpr_zqjlIEqUdbzbTaNBHwANsTSoZcoh51YO9MIn4VAcJABIe_eYybiSENJEYfwWKfIfbvJpMbW2g29sNBeaBA24DmgTddV3nJRdd5cGu6cz6Akuffrx5oxRqD6-u7_SrkT2xO1CdARxCjRxui1megc1YO7LVDF3ps=&c=y3xGYgXjbl0HukCuxJMD67gs67pObHFGpxhwVIVo-5gI3snVuAsB-w==&ch=TxfsJG48dUfdi8CnFVSguIcaqpNnTIVKv7ibh7_wxJ_hWM6IOEz2vQ==)
---
| Peter,
Earlier this year, MAGA billionaires thought they could buy the Wisconsin Supreme Court.
They poured millions into the race. They brought in national figures. They blanketed the airwaves.
And they lost.
They lost because Democrats started early, stayed united, and refused to let a judicial race fly under the radar. We didn't wait for the final weeks to respond -- we matched their energy from the start, educated voters about what was at stake, and turned out in force.
Now Pennsylvania is the next battlefield.
Three Democratic justices are up for retention this November. If MAGA flips those seats, they'll take control of the court and lock in a decade of voter suppression, gerrymandering, and unchecked right-wing legislation.
They've already started moving money into Pennsylvania -- quietly, so as not to repeat the mistakes of Wisconsin. The media isn't covering it. Most voters don't even know it's happening.
But we do. And we know how to win this fight.
Defend Our Courts is on the ground early, building the campaign that will keep Pennsylvania's highest court blue -- but we can't do it without you.
[Chip in $10, $25, or $50 today so we can put the Wisconsin playbook to work in Pennsylvania. Without the support of grassroots donors like you we are POWERLESS to stop MAGA Extremism... ](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m_VCtkjlGYcymjXbTG00wzRkoLgvGvNpa7zuiv6mSX9w541GgA7Rkc9gn30x3F0LRD89ZrpYthpr_zqjlIEqUdbzbTaNBHwANsTSoZcoh51YO9MIn4VAcJABIe_eYybiSENJEYfwWKfIfbvJpMbW2g29sNBeaBA24DmgTddV3nJRdd5cGu6cz6Akuffrx5oxRqD6-u7_SrkT2xO1CdARxLafRmRPGG5ZNKsqYZFLDyc=&c=y3xGYgXjbl0HukCuxJMD67gs67pObHFGpxhwVIVo-5gI3snVuAsB-w==&ch=TxfsJG48dUfdi8CnFVSguIcaqpNnTIVKv7ibh7_wxJ_hWM6IOEz2vQ==)

---
| [DONATE NOW TO STOP MAGA EXTREMISM!](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m_VCtkjlGYcymjXbTG00wzRkoLgvGvNpa7zuiv6mSX9w541GgA7Rkc9gn30x3F0LRD89ZrpYthpr_zqjlIEqUdbzbTaNBHwANsTSoZcoh51YO9MIn4VAcJABIe_eYybiSENJEYfwWKfIfbvJpMbW2g29sNBeaBA24DmgTddV3nJRdd5cGu6cz6Akuffrx5oxRqD6-u7_SrkT2xO1CdARxFsvgJMMj7ZQYldtrOp5Rq8=&c=y3xGYgXjbl0HukCuxJMD67gs67pObHFGpxhwVIVo-5gI3snVuAsB-w==&ch=TxfsJG48dUfdi8CnFVSguIcaqpNnTIVKv7ibh7_wxJ_hWM6IOEz2vQ==)

clean_body: They’ve already started moving money into Pennsylvania — quietly, so as not to repeat the mistakes of Wisconsin. The media isn’t covering it. Most voters don’t even know it’s happening. | | | | | == | Peter, Earlier this year, MAGA billionaires thought they could buy the Wisconsin Supreme Court. They poured millions into the race. They brought in national figures. They blanketed the airwaves. And they lost. They lost because Democrats started early, stayed united, and refused to let a judicial race fly under the radar. We didn't wait for the final weeks to respond -- we matched their energy from the start, educated voters about what was at stake, and turned out in force. Now Pennsylvania is the next battlefield. Three Democratic justices are up for retention this November. If MAGA flips those seats, they'll take control of the court and lock in a decade of voter suppression, gerrymandering, and unchecked right-wing legislation. They've already started moving money into Pennsylvania -- quietly, so as not to repeat the mistakes of Wisconsin. The media isn't covering it. Most voters don't even know it's happening. But we do. And we know how to win this fight. Defend Our Courts is on the ground early, building the campaign that will keep Pennsylvania's highest court blue -- but we can't do it without you. == | == | | | | We stopped them before. We can stop them again. Let's get to work. Team Defend Our Courts | | | | | | | | | | == | | | | | == | | | | Mail checks to: | | | | Defend Our CourtsP.O. Box 45569 Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | ==

name: TN House Democratic Caucus
subject: Sky-high grocery bills & soaring rent? Here’s the fix.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
Food prices are up 3%. Rents are still breaking records. Let’s deliver relief—together.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K2HQJQK1JXPA4JN8ENYGN902_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K2HQJQK1JXPA4JN8ENYGN902_1)
---
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K2HQJQK1JXPA4JN8ENYGN902_2)
---
| Dear Peter, From the checkout lane to the leasing office, costs keep climbing. **USDA data shows food prices are 3% higher than a year ago and still rising**. Meanwhile, making rent affordable is among Nashville residents’ very top priorities at **67%**. Trickle down policies don’t work and families can’t afford to wait any longer for real leadership.
House Democrats are championing a Tennessee Families First agenda: eliminating grocery taxes, increasing the minimum wage, capping insulin at $35, eliminating junk fees, investing in the Housing Trust Fund, and empowering local governments to meet demands for affordable housing. We’ve already built a bipartisan coalition to move the Affordable Housing and Tenant Protection Act out of committee—and we’re not stopping there!
But lobbyists for hedge funds and corporate landlords are flooding Nashville. Your $10, $35, or $100 contribution keeps our organizers knocking doors and our policy team drafting stronger consumer-protection language. Pitch in now and turn cost-of-living frustration into real-world progress.
---

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: The top Republican pollster just sounded the alarm for 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC
body:
The good news? It’s not the issues...
| **_A SECURE MEMO FROM HOUSE GOP LEADERSHIP_**
---
**Authorized by:**
---
[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=05eee23f5a62438b80dfc8447f15d679&_e=ihVB6zIRY-LzUrSc6GoR58bgfeAVNd4t9tR5_oxGXm2Kmr_97rXGwFkCdBVUksJEV8g05HFVpzbBMUme2SHornpWXobOanakYhp24CeMMVngSYPnK86vRyCzXrn_gLe3lH3Z2CVMvHOIAwNO-vxHpHz6MONRX2pjtysoQ6ObOPuvoZ8XjOu6d07-hIUasiAaMQGFxbx2LTpnyG9G-vjGMs3VipuBusFpmyeQS-CUK0qCmQrw7zPYxTlEIp428c9FllUCgaNcDZ5zjweN5Fq_sVHlzMMtFbEBgsrBj8DxtytcF7AtPGpYXag44A4Wt5EznNGcRmKIfslxmozja9IIJpOFIMuiOXFvucFMreDIrEacTt2jH0zftHf5xQmyo5eX4zuctCVA3YWGdhujbvSpG-Zz-1Axbv6oMp0oDDoi4QZR-5b1VBvHgIo_GNCdbWceAl48-dS0aR-M6HrfK9dY0uUVkayVdT_86uBTkwHSlXy2lnpp_RzcAQsbUfpBXgnaTe-hlUjbKWxbhZUIUe7jqauYNqcq9lNw7Ks2seUsKHA0tZwJ0luxpvv4CYBEpDFrgc9SLmJuU6d2AxSKVF6_ECf9AL3yWE-beIxRRU0j2ZM%3D)
---
Friend,
---
When I gave up my nomination to become Ambassador to the United Nations in order to remain in Congress and pass the President’s “Big Beautiful Bill,” I vowed to make sure that he keeps his House majority in the midterms.
---
**Given the massive sacrifice I made, you can bet I’m going ALL IN to make sure the President can enact as much of his America First agenda into law as possible...**
---
_...And he can’t do that without a Republican majority in Congress for _**ALL FOUR YEARS**_ of his second presidential term! _
---
That is why I was so alarmed when I saw new polling that shows us trailing the Democrats by **_3 POINTS_** on the generic ballot poll.
---
And I’m not the only one who’s sounding the alarm...
---
**_Trump pollster sounds alarm for Republicans in 2026_**
[Source: _NJ.com_]
---
The good news is that I don’t believe we’re losing on the issues. Polling shows the Democrats have a record-low approval rating as they move further and further to the Left!
---
Instead, there are two things that show up in the polling that worry me: **__Voter apathy and being outspent by the Democrats’ political machine.__**
---
The latest reports show House Democrats are already raising record-breaking amounts of cash from lobbyists and special interests to _BUY_ the midterms, and it’s only going to get worse as we get closer to Election Day.
---
But I have the solution to both of those problems...
---
**In my capacity as Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership, I’ve approved a _500% MATCH_ for top grassroots conservatives to help us raise the funds we need to get every last Republican voter to the polls in the midterms and go toe-to-toe with the Democrats on the airwaves. **
---
If we do that, it won’t be a matter of merely defending our House majority – it will be a matter of how many more Democrat seats we FLIP!
---
[**So please, make a contribution of ANY amount to take advantage of this 500% matching opportunity and help us DEFEND the President’s House Majority._ **](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=05eee23f5a62438b80dfc8447f15d679&_e=ihVB6zIRY-LzUrSc6GoR58bgfeAVNd4t9tR5_oxGXm2Kmr_97rXGwFkCdBVUksJEV8g05HFVpzbBMUme2SHornpWXobOanakYhp24CeMMVngSYPnK86vRyCzXrn_gLe3lH3Z2CVMvHOIAwNO-vxHpHz6MONRX2pjtysoQ6ObOPuvoZ8XjOu6d07-hIUasiAaMQGFxbx2LTpnyG9G-vjGMkweZr3WJwsKiXKdwGHFYHbviV5jx9xx0uRWWYvSKWI2X6dw5MxImwry3qm9M1pmGJmN9NXHNP3R7B6QhPSbO6Z3lrayDllsscrNdU9avVJ18y_HZnk8bj3vFjBJs0wNiqiLtWdBo7zFnaPaFQFgi9zHqdlXcSYmUCfX8JvGJTPzPI5PgzGIIwWSAA17WwfY04RcF1kXQJwwY4m4NKnpNC_yDTsr7zwldPsIvaXHt4M4ssO6xANWvEwd7PVAO3-JW-NFqPfEFSbE3i9p3vQGNLGvHVsHJvx7-vZaERp2incCF4McKbo4EO-BGWo25ufvFH6fe84kcYhsZD0yPXx2exsZFiJClVKmylGilrnOfJBEPWGXzYxrqfR-ghIMeuLNdz4ZMb5KQWYeub2o2czPENs%3D)

clean_body: The good news? It’s not the issues... | A SECURE MEMO FROM HOUSE GOP LEADERSHIP Authorized by: Friend, When I gave up my nomination to become Ambassador to the United Nations in order to remain in Congress and pass the President’s “Big Beautiful Bill,” I vowed to make sure that he keeps his House majority in the midterms. Given the massive sacrifice I made, you can bet I’m going ALL IN to make sure the President can enact as much of his America First agenda into law as possible... ...And he can’t do that without a Republican majority in Congress for ALL FOUR YEARS of his second presidential term! That is why I was so alarmed when I saw new polling that shows us trailing the Democrats by 3 POINTS on the generic ballot poll. And I’m not the only one who’s sounding the alarm... Trump pollster sounds alarm for Republicans in 2026 [Source: NJ.com] The good news is that I don’t believe we’re losing on the issues. Polling shows the Democrats have a record-low approval rating as they move further and further to the Left! Instead, there are two things that show up in the polling that worry me: Voter apathy and being outspent by the Democrats’ political machine. The latest reports show House Democrats are already raising record-breaking amounts of cash from lobbyists and special interests to BUY the midterms, and it’s only going to get worse as we get closer to Election Day. But I have the solution to both of those problems... In my capacity as Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership, I’ve approved a 500% MATCH for top grassroots conservatives to help us raise the funds we need to get every last Republican voter to the polls in the midterms and go toe-to-toe with the Democrats on the airwaves. If we do that, it won’t be a matter of merely defending our House majority – it will be a matter of how many more Democrat seats we FLIP! Thank you, Rep. Elise Stefanik Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. www.elisestefanik.com PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23281%2E4449941%2E-sqeAw) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23281%2E4449941%2E-sqeAw) | | It is with a heavy heart that we email you today.

It has been 15 years since _Citizens United_ began its reign of terror on our democracy.

**[So, we’re RUSHING to raise $25,000 before 11:59 PM to see this Amendment through.](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/66019?t=3&akid=23281%2E4449941%2E-sqeAw)**

| [2OO%-MATCH: RUSH $10 NOW TO END _CITIZENS UNITED_ >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/66019?t=4&akid=23281%2E4449941%2E-sqeAw)
---
Friend, we’ve begged you to help us overturn _Citizens United_. We pleaded with you. And still...nothing.

In the past 36 hours, our donations for a campaign to pass Amy Klobuchar and Adam Schiff’s Constitutional Amendment to ban Dark Money have reached an abrupt halt.
**_We missed our strict goal. Donald Trump is LAUGHING at us._**
**But we just received great news** : The deadline has been extended until the END OF THE DAY – and all donations will be 2OO% MATCHED!!

**But the harsh reality is:** If we don’t fill our $19,523 gap by tonight, our dreams of ending Dark Money’s iron grip on our elections will be crushed.

And if our dreams are crushed, Trump’s Republicans will sweep the Midterm Elections next year!
[**So, we’re begging on our hands and knees. Will you rush a 2OO%-MATCHED donation before it’s too late ?**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/66019?t=5&akid=23281%2E4449941%2E-sqeAw)

Donald Trump is LAUGHING at us. But we just received great news : The deadline has been extended until the END OF THE DAY – and all donations will be 2OO% MATCHED!! But the harsh reality is: If we don’t fill our $19,523 gap by tonight, our dreams of ending Dark Money’s iron grip on our elections will be crushed. name: Andrew Ollis, Kash Foundation
subject: The proof is out: Soros. Obama. Brennan. Comey.
body:
The Russiagate hoax wasn’t just political theater. It was a premeditated operation.

|
---
| | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z49r8hqfi52vbl6ftd2hul28qkkqjmgh4k9j1uu0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5chg6apj4cth2bs)
Patriot,

The newly declassified appendix from the Durham Report — released by Senator Chuck Grassley — confirms what we at the Kash Foundation have said all along:

**The Russiagate hoax wasn’t just political theater. It was a premeditated operation.**

According to the appendix, George Soros’s team, along with Barack Obama, John Brennan, and James Comey, were briefed on the plan to fabricate the Trump-Russia “collusion” narrative four days before the FBI even opened its investigation.

_They knew it was a lie.
They went along with it anyway.
**And they weaponized the most powerful institutions in America.**_

The Kash Foundation was created after Russiagate to keep the Deep State from continuing to operate in the shadows.

Now that the Government Gangsters have been exposed, we’re on a mission to rebuild trust and support in our institutions, particularly our law enforcement.

**In short, BACK THE BRAVE, BUILD THE FUTURE.**

You can take a stand for transparency and the future of our country by donating today.
[DONATE](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zqr77d974o6l19l4bn5q38ajcmnvoaatl58ogc3o_rp22sh2s8i6epb5chg6apj4cth2bs)
The Kash Foundation's goal is to create a healthier relationship between the US federal government and American citizens.

**Our mission is to support educational and legal efforts needed to facilitate government transparency.**

Right now, we’re at a critical moment.

They thought this would never come to light, but we never stopped fighting.

**Now, the truth is finally being exposed, and we must press forward.**

However, we still have a long way to go to bring trust back to our institutions. With your help, we’re not backing down.

[Please chip in $25, $50, or even $100 today to support our fight for transparency and accountability.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zbcg9s1iqvgvb54ca9v0sg5tqhk1gqs144jutmh0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5chg6apj4cth2bs)

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Sign the petition to stop Trump’s misuse of the national guard
body:
Once again Trump is targeting a majority-Democratic city | | |
---
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQCU9_O_YvL4doFtnquDjWjiD1inRkkt8pe7ollvKx9daVHW-J-wCSMMgqNCwMWGNV4LYqIycPinYavUb-6mVWvJvjhkhgu4XkDG2T4uEwGLnM6lta__u4QEaT89nszo1Pr3onarR-J4hpVdyVwrfdAxy-YjWCzNdzFjYqvTs8o9DwK_RQCBvAToSViNnoMmNq9i0aSJjUjJKjWzr-fjD4vXCM0vHCXJ0q64x1Wu_epEM2zfwuzo9lOY3r38k8GaWHocPis5ZA-pR9_2lkGs13JStCbWLeRM1357Jde_Ed9xcngvDZyIdol3mJbJyqYli-KpFzKtplFHxzU57aqrcs8152bDdRhmt3jfArRm018_ngvOi133I6CbqZjAHOxhVQ/4j0/GYx9VrMqSB2DuLhL9LefoQ/h0/h001.o5PCNVd1Ki1kW5j_1rp_dgAlxk8Q1CjlXPEqLJ-s8FM)Hi Friend, News broke Monday morning that Donald Trump plans to deploy the National Guard to Washington, D.C. Once again, he is targeting a majority-Democratic city, bypassing local leaders, and using federal troops as a show of force. We’ve seen this before. Earlier this year, Trump sent National Guard troops and active-duty Marines into Los Angeles without Governor Gavin Newsom’s consent. A federal judge ruled that action unconstitutional and outside his legal authority. The case is still in the courts, yet Trump is doubling down. [**Stop Trump's Misuse
of the National Guard**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQCU9_O_YvL4doFtnquDjWjiD1inRkkt8pe7ollvKx9daVHW-J-wCSMMgqNCwMWGNXXnXBxpkW5PUbHYBjq3vrfY06MclDxQLms--tj_k3HeE2LyxdjaHeyNyx0wGAYVX3tZefMT4ygjsNmVNnmN73iRzSqit1GzpXs10Zab4GwZEadkGNp_gPpcvl1vEKQeleT7RTAvTufTeyK9Cq4KCp_ErrX9tc0bIAlulrebbXyMVk-_PeL_hohtr-cIowO33jRMN_cfAL9Hhyh145SWBt0nEUxXXwp73PfJJgxUKQ_JsHO8Vig9UfZAw39Q3dxraY7NO2f4v1Zf38nUVbzm-kOm7mShzNJFq1OU9x696zPXwbK6ka7CD50xrKTf_2zIBc/4j0/GYx9VrMqSB2DuLhL9LefoQ/h1/h001.rDj4FNv4T0KPTImo5OlrDW2F0VgCmBOXjlDfXbBcxqQ)
Congress must act immediately. Lawmakers have both the constitutional power and the responsibility to check a lawless executive. They must launch oversight hearings, subpoena all related documents, and pass legislation to strictly limit domestic troop deployments. **** [**Tell Congress: Stop Trump from deploying the national guard for political purposes. Click here to sign the petition.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQCU9_O_YvL4doFtnquDjWjiD1inRkkt8pe7ollvKx9daVHW-J-wCSMMgqNCwMWGNXPECNETidaNTN1l198Mc1l3DuHM5bEyi3aJRdCaq866B8HvUSziZHy00A3nBDGnGYHAmsUspKXEMogi-W6HbbmrhSjrwjBjXu910iUg1nzNHl386bXhLFrskpW80OUiuGTlUEbUn2zDDI34WJmR8IMQZwAq1Hb_O6YdYhRgxDXA9JJ5vvOOL9kXb-qzYjWXU1mc-OHOuomqTtx9vGOFrkmOahYAJfcwurJJRNiztjA0fCO6GoEyIeX5JvZ3Emt67KrfR07Uap9cFV_frkC0taQfhvbuoKW1wJrhAFT77DjDUJ84WAvOzLSFx5LA4dWSE0/4j0/GYx9VrMqSB2DuLhL9LefoQ/h2/h001.ZmLWcUADhlaltRvqdxovXfx9_OgpzGBQyUF_Roy8TlY) In D.C., Trump's threats go even further. He has talked about evicting unhoused residents, hinted at stripping the city’s Home Rule, and smeared the capital as "totally out of control." This is about punishing political opponents and intimidating voters—not solving real problems. Deploying the military against American citizens is a line we cannot allow any president to cross. It erodes the separation of powers, undermines local authority, and violates the principles behind the Posse Comitatus Act. **[We cannot allow the National Guard to become a partisan weapon. Congress must stop Trump’s misuse of military force before it becomes the new normal. Sign our petition if you agree.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQCU9_O_YvL4doFtnquDjWjiD1inRkkt8pe7ollvKx9daVHW-J-wCSMMgqNCwMWGNV32mLhAuZwMEjNDoa5xKYB_utqGE6YXoSQVGkd-8lWYv61jrFqiasqcAVq6-Nh0Fuzsowqm9IcTJYgzxrw-6KMhfmCOPUWM4ZCc3ngi9jA-BgLpmAx9c1i2J_96J1QWdHsTxp_w7gFRPy-7qAi9UiWYELDhdcSQmOCK_NdzfFdP01QDYoKud45q-YD6UUh20oPU5Y2VyJqLcwcrayo7KtBEj9i9hATpixOMbLAvrQvoIiHHxZ33ktgq9peU1UbkHv2EbVe7E8lkXv8Jvj6NVNV9adDIOVoyfV7bk-HZUcMfazwT0FTO_qHycA3xusuD8s/4j0/GYx9VrMqSB2DuLhL9LefoQ/h3/h001.2Ngg-g9CfD64zSESJHAbD0mM1sDNaHxGz-OrWlKmqFg)** Thanks for everything you do. Josh
