name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: Extraordinary steps must be taken
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
My thoughts on this watershed moment for democracy.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113273411/563221571/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIzMDIxODVmLTk5NzgtZjAxMS1iNDgxLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=QuEqwCZVoNfUWwT5bxLr7zlRyUIvOWp5NhPzNNxnHII=&emci=bb342f0b-8978-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b302185f-9978-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, From the day President Obama and I started the National Democratic Redistricting Committee eight years ago, our north star has been fairness in the redistricting process. We oppose the use of partisan and racial gerrymanders — by all political parties — designed to disenfranchise people. I’m still deeply committed to that ideal. **But let me be clear: That enduring commitment to fairness does not blind us to reality.** Right now, Republican leaders, led by the president, are attempting to gerrymander their way to unchecked power. Let’s call this what it is: We are witnessing an authoritarian attempt to thwart the will of the people. We cannot stand by in the face of an overt threat to democracy. Extraordinary steps must be taken to meet this moment. **We must preserve our democracy today in order to ultimately heal it.** For that reason, we are evolving our approach. The NDRC will not oppose — on a temporary basis — responsible, responsive actions to ensure that the foundations of our democracy are not permanently eroded. And once this moment has passed, we must pass reforms at the federal and state levels to outlaw the very actions that have led to this crisis. The people who are willing to undermine the core precepts of democracy for partisan gain must never again be allowed to threaten what makes America exceptional. We are not abandoning the fight for fairness — far from it. Fairness is a weapon that exists to protect the people’s power. And we shouldn’t be afraid to use it. In solidarity, Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113273412/563221572/1256196342?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGHolderRR_email_20250813-NDRC-Tier1-AGHolderRR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250813-NDRC-Tier1-AGHolderRR_&utm_campaign=AGHolderRR&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIzMDIxODVmLTk5NzgtZjAxMS1iNDgxLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=QuEqwCZVoNfUWwT5bxLr7zlRyUIvOWp5NhPzNNxnHII=)

name: Team Schrier, Campaign Update
subject: Before Kim heads back to D.C., she wants to hear *directly* from you:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Quick survey: What should Kim focus on next? [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113273561/563222038/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjZjMWFmMS05OTc4LWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TsavIMotOWx6egbzslv9egTNU3RRJcK3JBxz2kvlnZs=&emci=9fa02bea-6678-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=466c1af1-9978-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

Every August, Congress takes its annual recess.

For Kim, that means being back home here in the 8th District, spending time with her family, visiting small businesses, talking with you and your neighbors, and staying connected with what’s happening in our community.

This time is vital, and it's Kim’s chance to listen directly to you before heading back to Washington, D.C. Peter, the best way she can fight for you in Congress is to be up to date on your priorities. She wants to know what's working, what’s not, and what matters most to you right now.

That’s why we’ve put together a quick priorities survey so you can share your thoughts with Kim. It only takes a few minutes, and it makes a real difference in shaping how Kim represents our district in Congress.

name: Living at home
subject: Time to take on the housing crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Isaiah Martin for Congress
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109386/images/IRM_wrapper-email.jpg]
friend, hey — it’s Isaiah Martin.
Housing Crisis EmailHousing should be a right
Right now, more young adults are living with their parents than at any point since the Great Depression. The cost of buying or renting a home is out of control, and Donald Trump’s economic policies have made the crisis worse.
This is what happens when corporate landlords, Wall Street investors, and GOP politicians treat housing as a profit machine instead of a basic human right.
We need to build more homes, stop the corporate takeover of the market, and make housing affordable again. That means fighting back hard. Now.
Chip in today to take on Trump’s housing crisis
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin?]
Every dollar helps us build homes people can afford and stop corporate abuse.

body:
| | | ******Confederates or constituents?
** [**Rep. Jen Kiggans chose confederates. Chip in to our billboard campaign to us FIRE her!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXO6GNKSqHD4Sh0_Ptelsb82XYP2HnqnHVoAXq-il0B2co2TmjhzsB1PdRyL04ory2ox5P3_xPMlQ_yedrWHh8ILKpdUSeXQJnyQsY5OBOwXK3FS_VXfys5ypaaVBlEMbkSiJd1sHBqQQ8t-NZqZmGygcfkwQnKCGXYYFRDUaxP5wt_gTI6hXx_ASV9r-SV9i__K-JTpPhCnfkrDRDw4GrNQ8npasnxmNZjD2t9k18ubFL_vondJgUtNRg9N2DwC3_iHM4Cppbd_2vN-NVCjV5dRKrgzB0Ehb4H4SMr3FtFR8nPPg6k6MQs4yCpvIn-K6yGrt8vco01oe9f8ZaSJ_aLNt3cQMTV1QjjqRiu8NyZcgGVuk69bqhFcTiu2sCFlIdN/4j0/mMZ8gTMhT0KsYOnu0wDPMg/h0/h001.RvQ-7DG2nxuUF8ZcqAXctRb4wWKKjJtE9YFBDhDDzVE)

Not only was she the deciding vote to gut vital programs like SNAP and Medicaid, but she also voted to resurrect Confederate base names, effectively erasing the history and contributions of Black veterans. At Able Dems, we are dedicated to electing Democrats with disabilities and their allies who will stand up against Trump’s bigotry and his MAGA enablers. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. ** Kiggans refused to join a bipartisan effort aimed at honoring Virginia’s Black veterans and servicemembers, demonstrating her disregard for the very constituents she is supposed to represent. Instead of standing for justice and equality, she has chosen to align herself with the MAGA agenda, which includes supporting Secretary Hegseth's racist push. This misguided decision will cost taxpayers millions to change the names of these bases, a financial burden that could have been avoided had she chosen to act ethically. clean_body: We know it’s been a crazy month with lots of twists and turns. | = | Whoa, did you see this?! Just revised ratings from Sabato’s Crystal Ball confirm what we already knew: California is ground zero in the fight for control of the U.S. House. = = = See all the highlighted districts? Those are seats that Democrats currently hold in California that election experts at Sabato’s Crystal Ball think Republicans could flip from blue to red. ➡️ CA-13, CA-45, CA-21, CA-9, CA-47, CA-27, CA-49, CA-25 These are eight competitive seats — and we cannot afford to lose a single one if Democrats are going to have a chance at taking back the U.S. House. We know it’s been a crazy month with lots of twists and turns. These maps could even end up changing sometime in the future — but it’s our responsibility to not take our eye off the ball and be prepared to mobilize no matter what. = = = = = = Thank you so much, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. | | Hi, This is Brigadier General James Emory Mace, U.S. Army (Retired). _**Today, I write to you not as a soldier... _but as a father._**_ I'm a **Purple Heart** veteran, and I’ve seen battle. I’ve worn the uniform. I’ve buried friends who never made it home. **And I’ve felt the sting of a Nation that sometimes forgets what our sacrifices were for.** _**[But my daughter—Congresswoman Nancy Mace—never forgot.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/08uDhlz57-iCEWbL582sUKlS6O1f9emckDeP82_Zs2FGS0Y4B6z4qdRTKvjzMiYyy281T97od2vmGO0RwX6pIaH9ELme3G1yl2sCcXB3yMv4LdI_c257DwZ03a4I66EmmUliBDJ_mNMfrQVJbPVfGbUm9rlS3fkousqtN20weoGSte6jACR-6DYd3U75m9moIat7iTW3X9SNrnh7zCUb_eANiaXKp2LkZPNojTyHcViYm5sRMBb3nZCza7dcplXt3x2_Gkwh0fuvOv4VSqumQwGF5NkChS4jJeQu79yh59x4EjbH2h_7uMOySJmIkNvoj-jrXkV4KuanJCzJIUTHWpQKL7n3eRWmb-PSDHSus-V9jJOB1xrBzMnPIF5l-dyceVOEapJUnmXaspxY4EB94eQNLRKolsILjJ6PAbxEGICWgd0J9rsdRH_0jvz4ra2rNnvXAN3jWJWE2zFfbLZgeyRbZIpEFLyrAtbkWqPLs2k9JVRE6wpuen5-sflpDs0A4MATNkhcQc4ijNLj3ObIPK5WP2UijQCPJ4ocBiHVA3YnF-pdC9KSzNQdbuG8Egw3lL_xG3yZ2slJC4Yh_jpzRH9ZXwLPWzH_du4hUcGz-_VVm4CijwnLRGAJ-ccyW19uEESqGU7Avzt_nPQ_fxW0MvaswFmP54QL01mM4MY_NotvvtNW3WKjuSatYiH7wPmCEcE0XMh86otQgTMrI_qEVKMtoim9pQoDiSwI66awYTkzl07UL7sq2lvbK-UVEHtzNXnb8Y_cjuo8htHbgfbVT1LML_hYmOnlOzTp-NtRArq_NGzVx1uOzcRE6fCfIpAhZ4IEHeP1i_zEuPeTK9H9woxLFhwPySKVQaBJjSJ0rQwGeZnTs7pnTVpcQqS663t7k0TG/8pifHW3ycBj3lR9HiOUjGknvVQCVddrP/11)**_ Nancy was raised in the shadow of the flag. When Nancy was 17, she dropped out of high school. Most kids in that situation fall apart. But not Nancy.**She picked herself up, took a job at Waffle House, and fought her way back.** **By 21, she became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s Military College. **High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | Hi, This is Brigadier General James Emory Mace, U.S. Army (Retired). Today, I write to you not as a soldier... but as a father. I'm a Purple Heart veteran, and I’ve seen battle. I’ve worn the uniform. I’ve buried friends who never made it home. And I’ve felt the sting of a Nation that sometimes forgets what our sacrifices were for. Nancy was raised in the shadow of the flag. She saw the uniform. She lived with the deployments. She knew the cost. And instead of running from it, she ran toward it. When Nancy was 17, she dropped out of high school. Most kids in that situation fall apart. But not Nancy.She picked herself up, took a job at Waffle House, and fought her way back. By 21, she became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s Military College. A place I knew well. Nancy then made it to Congress, where she represented the people of South Carolina’s First District. She’s been fighting her whole life; for herself, for her kids, for the truth, and now...for her state. It’s hard sitting back as a father and hearing the terrible things the media and the establishment say about her. I want to ask you something from one parent, one patriot, to another: She’s up against a machine that wants to erase the values of faith-based, service-minded Americans who believe in truth, family, and country. Nancy stood up for me. She stood up for EVERY veteran who was forgotten. She stood up when sanctuary sheriffs released illegal alien criminals onto South Carolina streets. She stood up when they tried to let biological men compete in women’s sports. She stood up when other Republicans stayed silent. And now, she’s standing up for YOU. But she needs your backup. Will you answer it with me today? Nancy’s not running to make friends. She’s running to win. To fight. To serve. That’s what she learned growing up in our home. That’s what she learned watching me go off to war. And that’s what she’ll carry with her to the Governor’s Mansion. Please, on this solemn day, join me in standing with my daughter. This is the 5th part of a 5 Part Series on Dismantling the Democrat Atom Bomb about the Big Beautiful Bill and its "devastating" impact on healthcare.
_For Part 1-**The Medicaid Paradox - Growth, Costs, and Misleading "Cuts"** \- [Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/8ea793f5-3f2e-4318-9214-989a737c9db9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
For Part 2: _**The Unjust Burden - How the Current System Fails Working Americans **[Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/8eda8383-f3a3-4407-8e0d-22cb67076b2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
For Part 3: _**The Monopoly Problem - How Consolidation Drives Up Healthcare Costs **[Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/90a3c19c-fbbd-4818-91b3-cfdd66bdf05b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
For Part 4: **The Human Cost - Physician Burnout and Dissatisfaction [Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/49632e30-ea57-45e5-a900-5f66e11e7e76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
## Part 5: The Path to Reform - Competition, Choice, and Patient-Centered Care
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0e72e0e8-7888-4d05-8928-99b6c8a83e03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As this initial series concludes, there are some serious challenges facing our healthcare system. The Medicaid cuts try to stop a problem and halt the Obamacare Affordable Care Act that would get Linda Richman from SNL Coffee Talk _Faklempt_ as _**it was neither Affordable, or Care or an Act!**_
[ Pledge your support](https://substack..K_5a-HQSIo9AAZk7Juo2ujfAb8Yafg8Ih6KvK1nn_RY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=170176996)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=170176996&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTc2OTk2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTUxMjg4MjMsImV4cCI6MTc1NzcyMDgyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ewdGLAfk4H0hIGyxveYa9PGZAWc86Rn6WbOm3WbwlDU)
There is hope for a more effective, affordable, system that restores physicians to the practice of medicine and adhering to the Hippocratic oath over insurance company denials and hospital and Private Equity monopolies. The solution is not minor course correction but a fundamental shift that restores competition, patient choice, and rebuilding the physician-patient relationship that is free from burdensome administration, corporate interference and misleading social media. Without this shift and restored enthusiasm for the next generation of physicians, the addition of so baby boomers will be a cataclysmic event.
**Refocusing Efforts to the Core Mission:** The primary goal of healthcare must be to enabling physicians to utilize their skills and knowledge to treat patients without bureaucracy and administrative burdens. The current system is over-regulated, and has turned healthcare to an impersonal interaction like a queue for customer service at your cable company from the patient experience and a miserable "factory" for the doctor who is reduced to an assembly-line worker with patients as the product. It also stops the physician from utilizing deductive reasoning and thinking out of the box to treat on their experience rather than run tests to satisfy some formula for their hospital grading system. Before this factory method, diagnostic testing was based on the human interaction and tests were not the first step before patient interaction. This paradigm must be reversed to prioritize individual patient care. At the same time, the modern patient intake form has expanded and is focused on irrelevant information. **Your sexual orientation is irrelevant for a broken arm but its on the form.**
**Competition Built this country and Choice can Restore Healthcare:** With the Hospital as the only game in town and single providers, higher costs and less client care is the result with reduced quality. Health care solutions must actively promote competition across the healthcare spectrum to:
* **Dismantle Monopolies:** Antitrust laws must change to dismantle the real monopolies. Otherwise we will have further consolidation. Some of these mega-systems need to be dismantled.
* **Empower Private Practice:** Regulation and administrative reform coupled with private practice initiatives should encourage and support independent physician practices and other lower-cost providers. These necessary changes will allow them to thrive while creating alternatives to large hospital systems. _**The private practice, if restored, can reduce ER intake, even in lower socio-economic communities.**_ Private Practices must be given the right to collectively bargain for their fees (_this includes the right to strike against an insurance company_) and not be forced to resort to acquisition by a hospital to make their practices profitable with the sacrifice of their independence.
* **Encourage Preventative Care** _**:**_ too much emphasis is on treatment versus prevention and insurance is structured in this manner. This systematic shift to preventative care will reduce the cost of long-term treatments.
* **Expose Healthcare Prices while Promoting Transparency:**_Requiring true price transparency for all healthcare services would empower consumers to make informed choices to lobby for alternatives while fostering competition among providers.**This is especially true for the Provider Tax that subsidizes hospital care.**_
* **Reduce Regulatory Burden:** Government regulation is not an invisible hand. It is a crippling noose that has stifled innovation and added significant administrative overhead. A streamlined regulatory environment would allow healthcare providers to operate more efficiently, reduce administrative costs, and focus more on patient care rather than compliance. This also means re-evaluating the provider tax system, which currently functions as a hidden tax on those with private insurance. The same is true for the FDA and bringing new cost-effective and preventative care treatments to the forefront.
* **Restore Trust and Autonomy:** Physician burnout is real and must be addressed along with new excitement to attract new talent to the profession. Efforts must be made to restore trust in the medical community (by reducing internet misinformation). Physicians need greater autonomy in how they practice; the robot practice must be eliminated to foster stronger patient-physician relationships, and ensure fair compensation to incentivize patient centered care that reduces total healthcare costs over KPIs and other institutional metrics in the volume-driven hospital models .

**These issues are Beyond Partisan Gamesmanship:** Healthcare affects all Americans and this bi-partisan divide must be eliminated. Diseases do not discriminate between Democrats, Republicans, and unaffiliated voters; they all get sick and need healthcare. The latest obsession with a 2% "Medicaid cut" is the red herring to distract the public from the real healthcare problems and the epidemics of disease plaguing this nation while patients are adversely affected by an overpriced, over-regulated, and a misdirected healthcare system that is anything but affordable. Why have healthcare costs gone up with no increase in patient outcomes. Economies of scale with mergers was supposed to reduce costs, but it hasn't indicating that a free-market does not exist in healthcare.
It is time to move beyond partisan rhetoric and embrace market-based solutions that prioritize competition, choice, and patient well-being to build a truly healthier future for all Americans. There is the way out to a new system that puts patients first.
**There is Hope**
According to a survey of physicians conducted by the Doctors Company a Nation Wide Physician Owned Malpractice Insurance Company, doctors still like medicine for the following reasons:
* Helping people recover from illnesses or injuries that have negatively impacted them (70%)
* The ability to solve complex and challenging problems (55%),
* Getting to utilize skills and knowledge that I worked hard to develop (52%),
* Working a collaborating with people who share the same passions as I do (43%)
* Getting to form personal connections with patients and their families(40%).

_**If physicians could focus on being doctors, if patients trusted their physicians, if medical care became more cost effective, if doctors could choose how they practiced, were fairly compensated and the bureaucratic noose around the medical profession were lifted we would have a better healthcare system.**_

name: Ghazala Hashmi
subject: tiny margins made all the difference
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Jones for Virginia
body:
Hi, it’s Ghazala Hashmi.
In June, I won the Democratic nomination for Virginia Lieutenant Governor by the tightest margin in recent memory — less than 1%.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones-hashmi_em_q32025? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones-hashmi_em_q32025?]
The results prove that elections can be won or lost on the tiniest of margins. And with the eyes of the nation turning to Virginia, we can’t afford to slow down at all in the run-up to November.
Ours is the only election in the country with a chance to flip a state from red to blue this year, and I don’t intend on wasting it.
As the eyes of the nation turn toward Virginia, I’m asking you to become a founding member of my campaign for Lieutenant Governor by splitting a donation of $20 or more between my General Election Fund and Jay Jones. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones-hashmi_em_q32025?amount=20]

This campaign is about the people — the working parents worried about affording childcare, the families struggling to find an affordable place to live, and the Virginians wondering who is fighting for them.
I won’t stop fighting, but I need you with me.
Thanks for your support,
Ghazala Hashmi
Democratic Nominee for Virginia Lieutenant Governor

clean_body: Hi, it’s Ghazala Hashmi. In June, I won the Democratic nomination for Virginia Lieutenant Governor by the tightest margin in recent memory — less than 1%. ? [?] The results prove that elections can be won or lost on the tiniest of margins. And with the eyes of the nation turning to Virginia, we can’t afford to slow down at all in the run-up to November. Ours is the only election in the country with a chance to flip a state from red to blue this year, and I don’t intend on wasting it. As the eyes of the nation turn toward Virginia, I’m asking you to become a founding member of my campaign for Lieutenant Governor by splitting a donation of $20 or more between my General Election Fund and Jay Jones. [] DONATE $20 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] DONATE $250 [] DONATE $500 [] DONATE MORE [?] This campaign is about the people — the working parents worried about affording childcare, the families struggling to find an affordable place to live, and the Virginians wondering who is fighting for them. name: Team DNC
subject: Texas is just the beginning
body:
---
**As Texas pushes redistricting plan to add 5 GOP House seats before 2026 elections, other states may follow** — _CBS News_ **[Pitch in to help Democrats fight back:](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250813_4290_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4290_22930333_B2uyhf&akid=4290%2E22930333%2EB2uyhf)** **[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250813_4290_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4290_22930333_B2uyhf&akid=4290%2E22930333%2EB2uyhf)** Derek, If you’ve been waiting for the right moment to make your first contribution to the DNC, we’re here to tell you, _this is it._ Here’s why: What’s happening in Texas should be a warning sign for all of us. Republicans are trying to redraw Texas’ map to give themselves FIVE new congressional seats. And this is just the first step in their plan. Trump and his allies are directing Republicans in states like Indiana, Missouri, and South Carolina to follow the same playbook: redraw their states’ maps and give themselves _more_ new seats. If they succeed, the costs could be devastating. Imagine another four years of Trump with ZERO guardrails — where Congress refuses to check his power and lets him do _anything he wants?_ We have to fight back, and we have to do it now. Democrats are organizing around the country to stop Trump’s redistricting plans and take back Congress, but to fund that work, we need your help today. Not tomorrow, not next week. Today is the time when your contribution will go the furthest. **[If protecting our democracy is worth $3 to you, that’s the contribution we’re asking you to make to the DNC today:](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250813_4290_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4290_22930333_B2uyhf&akid=4290%2E22930333%2EB2uyhf)**
